source.definition: To receive property, rights, or titles from someone who has died.

explanation.sourceText: The verb "inherit" comes from the Latin 'inhereditare', meaning 'to receive as an heir', derived from 'heres' meaning 'heir'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „inherit” provine din latină, din termenul 'inhereditare', care înseamnă 'a primi ca moștenitor', derivat din 'heres' ce înseamnă 'moștenitor'.

source.additional: Regular verb, conjugation: I inherit, you inherit, he/she inherits.

target.definition: A primi bunuri sau drepturi de la o persoană decedată.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a moșteni“ comes from Church Slavonic, from the verb „moštiti“ (to bequeath, to inherit), which derives from the Slavic root „mošt-„.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a moșteni” provine din slavonă, de la „moštiti” (a moșteni), derivat din rădăcina slavă „mošt-”.

target.additional: Regulă de conjugare: eu moștenesc, tu moștenești, el/ea moștenește.

examples.sourceText: She inherited a lot of money.
We inherit the rights and obligations.
He will inherit the family business.

learningTips.text: Remember 'inherit' sounds like 'in-her-it' — you receive something 'in' your family, 'her' or 'his' property.
The Romanian verb 'a moșteni' starts with 'moș', which means 'old man' — think of an old man passing down his belongings.
Think of 'moșteni' as 'moș' (old man) + 'teni' (to hold) — holding onto what is passed down.
Conjugation tip: 'eu moștenesc' ends with '-esc', a common ending for Romanian verbs in the first conjugation group.
